hypothesis: If tensor completion methods (CPD-S, NeAT, and their ensemble) are used as surrogate models, they will achieve higher predictive accuracy (mean R²) than Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP baselines for predicting Young's modulus (E) and mass-normalized Young's modulus (Ẽ), particularly when training data are limited or non-uniformly sampled, because tensor completion exploits the multi-way low-rank structure of the design parameter tensor.
